glossary.short: Published heart rate in beats per minute after validity gates.
glossary.long: This is the operator-facing HR value. Invalid or stale candidates are not promoted into the live KPI.
glossary.advanced: Usually comes from the phase-backed HR publish path, with raw or anchor states visible in audit fields.

glossary.short: Published respiration rate in breaths per minute after RR gates.
glossary.long: This is the operator-facing RR value. It can be blank when source, freshness, or policy gates fail.
glossary.advanced: RR uses phase, raw fallback, latch, and recovery diagnostics depending on current signal state.

glossary.short: Phase quality index for the heart-rate path.
glossary.long: Higher values mean the phase signal is more usable for HR estimation.
glossary.advanced: A low PQI can force publish suppression or make the ML verdict a signal-hygiene failure.

glossary.short: Phase quality index for the breathing path.
glossary.long: Higher values mean the breath phase is cleaner and more stable.
glossary.advanced: Used by RR gating and as part of signal-quality review.

glossary.short: Phase after slow drift and DC behavior are controlled.
glossary.long: The firmware and trainer prefer stabilized phase for robust vital-sign estimates.
glossary.advanced: Stabilization reduces low-frequency drift before spectral/autocorrelation decisions.

glossary.short: A recently trusted value retained during short signal lapses.
glossary.long: Anchors avoid flicker, but stale anchors are explicitly audited and can block publish.
glossary.advanced: Anchor age and drift are separate safety checks.

glossary.short: A warning that an anchor no longer agrees with current evidence.
glossary.long: Drift can indicate stale state or a changing subject/radar setup.
glossary.advanced: Anchor drift participates in truthfulness and review flags.

glossary.short: Uncorrected or lightly corrected raw heart-rate candidate.
glossary.long: Raw HR is useful for audits but is not automatically truthful enough for the main KPI.
glossary.advanced: Raw HR can contain RR harmonics, half-rate errors, or high-bias artifacts.

glossary.short: Current HR estimate before final publish policy.
glossary.long: A candidate can exist while the published value remains blank.
glossary.advanced: Candidate evidence is combined with PQI, freshness, anchors, and publish policy.

glossary.short: Final radar HR candidate written to the CSV before display validity is applied.
glossary.long: The dashboard only treats it as live when the logged-valid flag and freshness pass.
glossary.advanced: Preserving reported_hr while hiding invalid live HR is a truthfulness requirement.

glossary.short: Why a value was published or suppressed.
glossary.long: Publish reasons explain blank or stale-looking KPIs without hiding the underlying state.
glossary.advanced: Names map directly to HR_PUBLISH_REASON_NAMES and RR_PUBLISH_REASON_NAMES.

glossary.short: Why a candidate passed or failed a signal gate.
glossary.long: Gate reasons identify signal, motion, PQI, harmonic, and confidence failures.
glossary.advanced: Names map directly to HR_GATE_REASON_NAMES and RR_GATE_REASON_NAMES.

glossary.short: Current session state: absent, warmup, settling, locked, post-motion, or leaving.
glossary.long: Metrics are more trustworthy when the session is locked than during warmup/settling.
glossary.advanced: Phase subsets are reported separately in analysis gates.

glossary.short: Bitmask describing possible HR/RR harmonic relationships.
glossary.long: Harmonic contamination can make HR agree with the wrong frequency.
glossary.advanced: Used by raw-disagree and physiology limitation diagnostics.

glossary.short: Breakdown of raw HR disagreement causes.
glossary.long: Helps distinguish high bias, stale phase, anchor drift, and RR harmonic contamination.
glossary.advanced: Feeds the ML-readiness physiology limitation mapping.

glossary.short: Firmware/module/schema identity observed from logs and manifests.
glossary.long: A mismatch is shown prominently because it can invalidate the whole contract.
glossary.advanced: Includes sketch version, module version validity, contract length, and schema/scoring hashes.

glossary.short: Hash of the feature/schema identity used for analysis.
glossary.long: A changed schema can make sessions incomparable until reanalysis or retraining is done.
glossary.advanced: Compared with manifest identity for drift detection.

glossary.short: Oximeter perfusion index quality signal.
glossary.long: Low PI can make reference HR/RR unreliable even when packets arrive.
glossary.advanced: PI below the threshold maps to a reference-quality limitation.

glossary.short: Fraction of useful BLE reference packets retained for analysis.
glossary.long: Low coverage means the reference signal may not support ML training.
glossary.advanced: Coverage below 50 percent fails reference readiness; 70 percent is the ready target.

glossary.short: Root mean square error between radar and reference.
glossary.long: Lower is better; large errors are penalized strongly.
glossary.advanced: The primary HR gate uses RMSE below 8 bpm with correlation above 0.4.

glossary.short: Mean absolute error between radar and reference.
glossary.long: Lower is better and easier to interpret than RMSE.
glossary.advanced: MAE complements RMSE because it is less dominated by large outliers.

glossary.short: Average signed radar-minus-reference error.
glossary.long: Positive bias means radar is high; negative bias means radar is low.
glossary.advanced: Bias by rate bucket helps detect systematic raw-HR distortion.

glossary.short: Linear correlation between radar and reference.
glossary.long: Higher is better; near-zero or negative correlation means tracking is poor.
glossary.advanced: The primary ML gate requires r greater than 0.4.

glossary.short: Training-readiness rule based on HR r and RMSE.
glossary.long: A pass means the session is a strong candidate for ML training if reference and truthfulness checks also pass.
glossary.advanced: Primary gate is r > 0.4 and RMSE < 8 bpm, with enough valid pairs.

glossary.short: Coverage-penalized 10-minute HR gate.
glossary.long: This helps avoid sessions that only look good in a tiny subset.
glossary.advanced: Used as a supporting analysis check, not a replacement for truthfulness.

glossary.short: How recently a telemetry value was updated.
glossary.long: Old values are marked stale or blanked instead of being shown as live.
glossary.advanced: The dashboard hard-stale rule is intentionally conservative.

glossary.short: Automatic-gain-control behavior that can indicate poor signal dynamics.
glossary.long: A high AGC floor percentage is a signal hygiene warning.
glossary.advanced: Shown in analysis audit and report signal quality.

glossary.short: Radar reflection close enough to contaminate tracking.
glossary.long: Move reflective objects or adjust radar placement if this appears.
glossary.advanced: Contributes to AGC/anomaly review flags.

glossary.short: Frames where DSP was skipped or filled.
glossary.long: Some skip-DSP is tolerable, but sustained skip-DSP reduces confidence.
glossary.advanced: Shown in funnel and AGC anomaly summaries.

tooltips.kpi.hr: Published heart rate after logged-valid and freshness gates. Blank means the dashboard is refusing to make stale or invalid HR look live.
tooltips.kpi.rr: Published respiration rate after RR source, freshness, and policy gates.
tooltips.kpi.fps: Effective radar/DSP frame cadence. Very low FPS can make trend and freshness decisions less reliable.
tooltips.kpi.distance: Current target range from radar spatial telemetry.
tooltips.preflight.python_env: Checks required Python packages before hardware collection starts.
tooltips.preflight.firmware_file_present: Parses the firmware DATA header and verifies the expected v15.1 219-column contract while preserving the 207-column legacy prefix.
tooltips.preflight.serial_port_list: Lists serial ports and checks whether the selected radar port is present.
tooltips.preflight.serial_port_probe: Opens the selected radar port briefly and listens passively for boot/header evidence.
tooltips.preflight.ble_adapter: Exercises the Bluetooth adapter through Bleak discovery.
tooltips.preflight.ble_device_probe: Looks for the configured oximeter address without starting a full session.
tooltips.preflight.session_folder_writable: Verifies the sessions folder can be written.
tooltips.preflight.disk_space: Warns when session storage is low.
tooltips.preflight.schema_hash_consistency: Compares scoring/schema identity against prior sessions when available.
tooltips.preflight.clock_monotonic_sanity: Checks that the monotonic clock advances normally.
tooltips.verdict.ready: Ready means the ML gate, reference coverage, and truthfulness checks are acceptable.
tooltips.verdict.conditional: Conditional means the session may be useful, but needs a longer or cleaner run before training.
tooltips.verdict.not_ready: Not ready means at least one critical signal, reference, policy, data, or truthfulness condition failed.
tooltips.metric.rmse: Root mean square error. Lower is better; large misses are penalized strongly.
tooltips.metric.mae: Mean absolute error. Lower is better and easier to interpret than RMSE.
tooltips.metric.r: Pearson correlation between radar and reference.
tooltips.truth.sketch_fw: Firmware sketch version observed or recorded in the session manifest.
tooltips.truth.module_fw: mmWave module firmware version observed in logs.
tooltips.truth.module_valid: Whether the module firmware identity was considered valid.
tooltips.truth.schema_hash: Feature/schema hash used by analysis. Mismatches can invalidate comparisons.
tooltips.truth.scoring_weights_hash: Hash for scoring weights used by this analysis.
tooltips.reference.coverage: Useful BLE reference coverage retained for analysis.
tooltips.reference.pi: Oximeter perfusion index. Low PI can make the reference unreliable.
tooltips.reference.decode_error: BLE packets that could not be decoded cleanly.
tooltips.hist.gate: Top gate reasons. These explain why candidates were accepted or rejected.
tooltips.hist.publish: Top publish reasons. These explain why values were shown or suppressed.

faq.a: The dashboard blanks HR when the trainer says the value is invalid, stale, or policy-blocked. The raw reported value and publish reason remain visible in Audit.
Higher PQI is better. Very low PQI suggests poor phase quality and can make the ML verdict a signal-hygiene failure.
Warmup and settling are less reliable than locked. Reports show phase subsets so a good-looking average does not hide unstable periods.
A trust anchor is a recently accepted value retained during brief lapses. It is audited for freshness and drift.

faq.q: Why RMSE and not only MAE?
faq.a: RMSE penalizes large misses more strongly. MAE remains useful as a human-readable average error.
Radar collection continues, but reference coverage drops. Low coverage can make the session not ready for ML training.

faq.q: What if firmware does not match?
faq.a: Treat the session as not ready. The CSV contract and interpretation may be wrong until firmware/trainer/dashboard versions are aligned.

dsp_steps.title: Radar frames
dsp_steps.summary: The module emits radar frames and vital-sign telemetry.
dsp_steps.detail: Firmware logs target and phase-related fields into the fixed DATA CSV contract.

dsp_steps.title: Phase stabilization
dsp_steps.summary: Phase signals are stabilized before vital extraction.
dsp_steps.detail: Slow drift, fill-on-miss behavior, and skip-DSP state are tracked explicitly.

dsp_steps.title: Target tracking
dsp_steps.summary: A primary target is selected from range/spatial evidence.
dsp_steps.detail: Distance, point cloud, target-info, and Doppler diagnostics remain visible.

dsp_steps.title: HR candidates
dsp_steps.summary: Heart-rate candidates come from phase and raw/spectral evidence.
dsp_steps.detail: The trainer records candidate, raw, corrected raw, anchor, and final publish values.

dsp_steps.title: RR candidates
dsp_steps.summary: Respiration candidates come from breath phase and source selection.
dsp_steps.detail: RR audit fields show source, latch, recovery, and anchor state.

dsp_steps.title: PQI gates
dsp_steps.summary: PQI and confidence gates decide whether candidates are trustworthy.
dsp_steps.detail: Failed gates are kept as reason histograms rather than hidden.

dsp_steps.title: Trust anchors
dsp_steps.summary: Anchors bridge short lapses but are freshness-checked.
dsp_steps.detail: Stale anchors or anchor drift can suppress publish.

dsp_steps.title: Publish policy
dsp_steps.summary: Publish gates decide what the user sees.
dsp_steps.detail: The dashboard never turns suppressed values into live KPI values.

dsp_steps.title: BLE reference
dsp_steps.detail: Packet coverage, decode health, and perfusion index determine reference quality.

dsp_steps.title: Post-session analysis
dsp_steps.summary: Radar and reference are aligned and scored.
dsp_steps.detail: Reports show RMSE, MAE, bias, r, gates, histograms, truthfulness, and ML readiness.

troubleshooting.title: COM10 not found
steps: Check USB cable and power.
Open Device Manager and confirm the assigned COM port.
Select the detected port in the dashboard.

troubleshooting.title: BLE device not responding
steps: Enable Bluetooth.
Wake the oximeter.
Confirm the MAC address.
Retry BLE device probe.

troubleshooting.title: Bluetooth adapter error
steps: Enable the Windows Bluetooth adapter.
Restart Bluetooth support service if needed.
Retry preflight.

troubleshooting.title: Session folder not writable
steps: Choose a writable workspace.
Close programs holding files in the sessions folder.
Retry preflight.

troubleshooting.title: Firmware contract mismatch
steps: Use the v16.2.0 firmware source beside the trainer.
Reflash if the device emits a different header.
Do not train from mismatched sessions.

troubleshooting.title: No DATA rows during warmup
steps: Wait for the roughly 60 second calibration window.
Watch for BOOT and SENSOR lines.
Do not treat warmup silence as a hard failure by itself.

troubleshooting.title: Dashboard stuck waiting
steps: Confirm the control server is running.
Check active session status.
Open the session folder and inspect live_dashboard.json.
